turns.student: What brings you in today?
turns.patient: I've been having this persistent cough for two weeks now and I'm really struggling to breathe. It's just getting worse over time, and it's starting to affect my daily life, including work and sleep.

turns.student: Can you tell me more about what you've been feeling?
turns.patient: It's just this constant nagging cough that won't go away, and when I try to breathe deeply or exert myself even slightly, it gets really bad. Sometimes I feel like I'm choking on my own mucus, and it's hard to catch my breath, you know?

turns.student: When you have those episodes, do you notice anything else happening to your body?
turns.patient: Yeah... when I get these really bad coughing fits, I sometimes feel a little lightheaded or dizzy, but that usually goes away once I sit back and try to calm down. And just lately, I've been getting this low-grade fever, like 38-point-something degrees, that comes on in the evenings and sticks around until morning.

turns.student: Any sweating or clamminess when the symptoms hit?
turns.patient: Actually, yeah... I have noticed some night sweats lately. It's really weird because I'm not usually a hot sleeper, but for the past few nights, I've woken up drenched in sweat and my pajamas are always soaked by morning.

turns.student: So no perspiration at all during the episodes?
turns.patient: Actually, yeah... sometimes when I'm having one of those bad coughing fits, my face will get really hot and sweaty, but that's about it. It's not like a full-body sweat like with the night sweats, just mostly my face and upper body.

turns.student: Does the discomfort stay in one spot, or does it move around?
turns.patient: The coughing pain is usually on my right side, it feels sharp and gets worse when I breathe deeply or cough. Sometimes it radiates up to my shoulder, but the worst of it seems to be centered around my lower right chest.

turns.student: Does it radiate anywhere — like your arms, neck, or jaw?
turns.patient: The pain usually stays on my right side, and occasionally it will feel like it's wrapping around my upper back a bit, but nothing too severe. Sometimes when I'm coughing really hard, I'll get this weird sensation in my shoulders, like they're being pulled down or something, but that's about it.

turns.student: Any tingling or numbness in your arms when it happens?
turns.patient: No, nothing like that. It's mostly just a sharp pain on my right side, and sometimes I get this feeling of tightness in my chest, like my lungs are being squeezed.

turns.student: What about your extremities — any tingling, pain, or unusual sensations?
turns.patient: No, nothing like that. My arms and legs just feel normal when I'm having these episodes. But to be honest, it's really hard for me to think straight when I'm coughing like this - my mind feels foggy and I get a little worried that something more serious is going on.

turns.student: Have you noticed any swelling anywhere? Ankles, feet, hands?
turns.patient: No, I don't think so. Like, no noticeable swelling or anything like that. Just the coughing and shortness of breath, which is really making me worried about what's going on with my lungs.

turns.student: Do your shoes feel tighter than usual, or any puffiness in your legs?
turns.patient: No, I don't think so. Like I said, it's mostly just the coughing and shortness of breath that's really bothering me. To be honest, I'm getting a little concerned about what might be going on with my lungs - all these symptoms are making me a bit anxious.

turns.student: Any stomach issues during the episodes? Nausea, queasiness, upset stomach?
turns.patient: Yeah... actually, yeah. Sometimes when I have one of those really bad coughing fits, I'll feel nauseous and lightheaded at the same time. It's like my body is just overwhelmed by it all. And to be honest, that makes me a little worried about what might be going on internally - you know, if something's affecting my lungs and making me feel so weak and queasy.

turns.student: Do you feel sick to your stomach when the symptoms come on?
turns.patient: Yes, I do. It's like my body is just overwhelmed by it all, and sometimes I'll feel really nauseous during one of those bad coughing fits. And then after it passes, I might still feel a bit queasy for a while. But it's not always there - some days are better than others.

turns.student: Have you had any fevers, chills, or night sweats?
turns.patient: Yes, like I mentioned earlier, I've been getting this low-grade fever that comes on in the evenings and sticks around until morning. And yeah, I have been having these really bad night sweats lately, where I wake up drenched in sweat and my pajamas are soaked by morning. It's really weird because I'm not usually a hot sleeper.

turns.student: Have you noticed any changes in your appetite or weight?
turns.patient: I've definitely been feeling less hungry than usual over the past few days, like I just don't have the energy to eat much. And to be honest, I think I might have lost a bit of weight since this all started - my clothes feel looser on me, but it's hard for me to tell for sure because I haven't been weighing myself regularly.

turns.student: Tell me about your lifestyle — diet, exercise, daily routine.
turns.patient: I'm an elementary school teacher, so I work with young kids all day, which can be pretty demanding physically and emotionally. As for my diet, I try to eat pretty healthily - lots of fruits and veggies, whole grains... you know, the usual stuff. But since this cough started, I've been feeling really tired and just haven't had the energy to cook or even think about food, which is weird because I usually love cooking. Exercise-wise, I'm not super active outside of work, but I do try to get some light exercise in on the weekends, like yoga or a short walk with my partner. But lately, even those small things have been feeling really exhausting.

turns.student: How active are you physically? Do you work out or walk regularly?
turns.patient: I'm pretty active, but not extremely so. I work with young kids all day, which can be physically demanding, but I don't do any intense exercise outside of that. Like I said, on weekends, I'll usually try to get in some light yoga or a short walk with my partner. But since this cough started, even those small things have been feeling really exhausting.

turns.student: What does a typical day of eating look like for you?
turns.patient: To be honest, it's been really tough lately. I've just been grabbing whatever's easy to eat because I don't have the energy to cook or even think about food. So, it's been a lot of takeout, leftovers, and quick snacks like granola bars or fruit. My partner has been trying to help me stay on track, but it's hard when I'm just not feeling up to it. Usually, I eat pretty healthily - lots of fruits and veggies, whole grains... you know, the usual stuff. But since this cough started, everything feels like an effort, even eating.

turns.student: Any medical problems in your family? Parents, siblings?
turns.patient: Yeah, my mom actually has asthma, so I've grown up seeing her deal with respiratory issues. But aside from that, no major medical problems on either side of my family that I know of. My grandmother did pass away from lung cancer when I was a kid, but that's about it.

turns.student: Specifically, has anyone in your family had serious diseases?
turns.patient: My mom has asthma, like I mentioned before. That's the only real respiratory issue on my side of the family that I know of. But yeah, it's been kind of a concern for me lately, especially with all these symptoms I'm experiencing.
